schoolName: MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology)

sources.label: MCAST official site - About MCAST / Mission Statement
sources.notes: Confirms MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology) was established in 2001 and is Malta's leading vocational and higher education institution, with six/seven Institutes plus a Gozo Campus, offering over 190 full-time and 300+ part-time courses from MQF/EQF Level 1 to Level 8 (certificate to Doctoral level). States its address structure includes a Main Campus, an Institute for the Creative Arts campus in Mosta, a Gozo Campus, and the Institute of Applied Sciences (Centre of Agriculture, Aquatics and Animal Sciences) in Qormi/Luqa.

sources.label: MCAST official course page - Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing (AS6-W04-23)
sources.notes: Official programme page: 3-year, full-time, MQF/EQF Level 6, 180-ECTS Bachelor of Science (Hons) Nursing, offered by the Institute of Applied Sciences, Department of Applied Sciences, delivered at the Paola Campus per the course code's departmental listing (course is administratively under the Institute of Applied Sciences; teaching/placements for this award have historically run out of the Qormi/Luqa Centre of Agriculture, Aquatics and Animal Sciences site per other MCAST/search-result corroboration -- prospective students should confirm the exact current teaching location with MCAST directly). States the programme prepares learners to register as a first-level nurse in Malta and all EU member states, requiring a minimum of 4,600 hours over 3 years (2,300 theoretical + 2,300 practice hours) per EU Directive 2005/36/EC, with practice hours mostly on clinical placements supervised by qualified nurse mentors. States graduates must obtain a pass in all theoretical and practical units to apply for registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta, and that the course is recognised across all EU member states. Notes a values-based interview, occupational health-screening certificate, and clean police conduct certificate are required for admission; entry requirements include MCAST Advanced Diplomas in relevant health/social-care fields or 2 A-Level + 2 I-Level passes, with an international-applicant Preliminary Interview stage before the Value Based Interview, and a Maturity Clause route for applicants aged 23+.

sources.label: MCAST official site - International Students
sources.notes: Confirms MCAST has self-accrediting status awarded by the Malta National Commission for Higher and Further Education (MFHEA), that its degrees align with the European Qualifications Framework and are internationally recognised, and that Nursing is explicitly listed among MCAST's international-student programme areas. States Malta is bilingual (Maltese and English), about 100km from mainland Europe with international airport connections, and that MCAST hosts a growing international student community with programmes delivered in English. Page notes a last-update date of 20 March 2020, so some operational specifics should be reconfirmed directly with MCAST's International Office.

sources.label: MCAST official site - How to Apply
sources.notes: Referenced from the Nursing course page as the official application-process and FAQ resource for prospective students, confirming MCAST's standard online-application route for full-time programmes.

sources.label: Wikipedia - Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology
sources.notes: Independent tertiary source corroborating MCAST as a public vocational education institution established in 2001, consisting of seven institutes over four campuses (Paola Main Campus, Mosta, Qormi/Luqa, and Gozo), with approximately 6,500 full-time and 5,000 part-time students, motto 'Scientia et labore', and headquartered in Paola, Malta. Also confirms MCAST's Institute of Applied Sciences (which administers the Nursing degree) and notes MCAST's 2024 hosting of the EU4Dual conference at its Paola campus and 2021 international collaboration with a Norwegian secondary school, supporting MCAST's public-institution status and Paola-centred identity used in this draft.

sources.label: Northumbria University official course page - BSc (Hons) Nursing Studies (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology)
sources.notes: Independent official corroboration from MCAST's UK partner university describing a joint BSc (Hons) Nursing award and professional-registration programme between MCAST and Northumbria University, taught via classroom, clinical-skills suites, and clinical placement blocks in close collaboration with Malta and Gozo's health service providers, with academic staff at both MCAST and Northumbria being registered nurses. Confirms the Malta Nursing and Midwifery Council code of ethics (2020) is used as a curriculum framework, and that simulation facilities at MCAST are used for skills such as intravenous therapy delivery. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: this specific Northumbria-branded joint-award page states 'this course is no longer accepting new applications' and lists a start date of October 2021, indicating the Northumbria dual-award route (as distinct from MCAST's own standalone BSc (Hons) Nursing degree, which remains listed as active in MCAST's 2026/27 course catalogue) may have been paused, changed, or discontinued on the Northumbria side. This draft therefore does NOT claim an active Northumbria dual-award pathway and instructs applicants to verify current joint-award status directly with MCAST; the standalone MCAST degree leading to Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta registration is the pathway corroborated as currently active via MCAST's own official course catalogue.

sources.label: MG2i Malta (MCAST's commercial/training arm) - Undergraduate Certificate in Nursing Studies (Bridging Course) listing (via search-result summary; live fetch blocked by Cloudflare bot-challenge)
sources.notes: MG2i is described elsewhere on MCAST's own official About page as 'MCAST's commercial arm.' Search-result summary of this MG2i course listing describes a short (approximately 6-month) Nursing Studies Bridging Course intended for non-EU-qualified nurses who already hold a BSc in Nursing but need to cover specific missing learning outcomes required under EU Directive 2005/36/EC, covering modules such as Multidisciplinary Approaches in Health and Social Care, Quality Assurance in Health and Social Care, Ethical Issues and Decision Making, Work-Based Training in Health Science, Intermediate English, Gerontology, and Mental Health, with successful completers eligible to apply for registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta. Direct page fetch was blocked by a Cloudflare bot-challenge during this research pass, so this entry relies on the independent search-engine-cached summary of MG2i's own listing rather than a directly verified live page; applicants should reconfirm current bridging-course content, duration, fees, and intake directly with MG2i/MCAST before applying.

sources.label: Britannica - Paola, Malta
sources.notes: Independent encyclopedic corroboration of Paola as a town in Malta's South Eastern Region, providing general historical and geographic context (Paola was founded in the 17th century, is located near Malta's Grand Harbour area, and today functions as a residential and commercial town) used to ground this draft's city-profile narrative.

structuredFacts.name: MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology)
bestFitFor: Students who want an EU-recognised, English-medium Bachelor of Science (Hons) Nursing degree from a public, self-accrediting Maltese institution, and who are comfortable completing the full programme's 4,600 hours (2,300 theory + 2,300 supervised clinical placement) over 3 years as required by EU Directive 2005/36/EC

Students targeting eventual registration with Malta's Nursing and Midwifery Council and EU-wide nurse recognition, who understand that separate Indian Nursing Council (INC) equivalence for any foreign nursing qualification is assessed case-by-case (via INC's Equivalence Committee process) rather than automatic, and who are prepared to independently verify INC's specific treatment of an MCAST qualification before enrolling

Non-EU-qualified nurses who already hold a BSc in Nursing and are specifically looking for a short bridging-style pathway to cover EU Directive 2005/36/EC learning-outcome gaps, who will independently reconfirm the current bridging course's content, fees, and intake directly with MCAST/MG2i since this could not be fully verified on a live official page during this research pass

Applicants comfortable with MCAST's values-based, multi-stage admissions process (values-based interview, international-applicant preliminary interview, occupational health screening, and clean police conduct certificate) as a mandatory gateway into clinical placements

Students who want to be based in Malta's compact, English/Maltese-bilingual EU island environment, centred on MCAST's Paola main campus and its Institute of Applied Sciences programmes, rather than a large multi-faculty university city campus

programs.title: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing
programs.notes: MQF/EQF Level 6, 180-ECTS, 3-year full-time degree (course code AS6-W04-23) under MCAST's Institute of Applied Sciences, Department of Applied Sciences. Requires a minimum of 4,600 hours over 3 years (2,300 theoretical + 2,300 clinical-practice hours) per EU Directive 2005/36/EC, most practice hours completed on supervised clinical placements with qualified nurse mentors. Graduates who pass all theoretical and practical units may apply for registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta; MCAST's official page states the qualification is recognised across all EU member states. Admission requires a values-based interview (with an added Preliminary Interview stage for international applicants), an occupational health-screening certificate, and a clean police conduct certificate, plus either specific MCAST Advanced Diplomas (Health Sciences, Health and Social Care, Social Care, Dental Surgery Assistants, or Pharmacy Technicians) or 2 A-Level + 2 I-Level passes (a Maturity Clause route exists for applicants aged 23+). IMPORTANT CAVEAT: MCAST also runs (or has run) a joint BSc (Hons) Nursing Studies dual-award with UK partner Northumbria University; Northumbria's own official page for that specific joint-award route states it is 'no longer accepting new applications' (last listed intake October 2021). This draft covers MCAST's own standalone BSc (Hons) Nursing degree, which remains listed as active in MCAST's current course catalogue, and does not claim the Northumbria dual-award route is currently open -- applicants interested specifically in a UK dual-award pathway must verify current status directly with MCAST. No tuition fee for EU/Malta-domiciled or international/non-EU students could be corroborated on MCAST's own official pages during this research pass, so no fee figure is stated; applicants must obtain current fee information directly from MCAST's International Office.

programs.title: Undergraduate Certificate in Nursing Studies (Bridging Course)
programs.notes: Short bridging-style certificate (independent search-result summaries describe roughly 6 months' duration; this could not be independently confirmed on a live page during this research pass because the source page was blocked by a Cloudflare bot-challenge) run via MG2i, described on MCAST's own About page as 'MCAST's commercial arm.' Intended for non-EU-qualified nurses who already hold a BSc in Nursing Studies but need to cover specific missing learning outcomes required under EU Directive 2005/36/EC, with modules reported to include Multidisciplinary Approaches in Health and Social Care, Quality Assurance in Health and Social Care, Ethical Issues and Decision Making, Work-Based Training in Health Science, Intermediate English, Gerontology, and Mental Health. Successful completers are reported to be eligible to apply for registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta. Duration is set to 1 year in this record only as a schema placeholder for a program shorter than a full academic year; applicants MUST reconfirm the actual duration, current curriculum, entry requirements, fees, and intake dates directly with MG2i/MCAST before applying, since this could not be verified on a live official page in this research pass.

structuredFacts.researchNotes: MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology) is a public vocational and higher-education institution established in 2001, independently corroborated by Wikipedia as consisting of seven institutes across four campuses (Paola Main Campus, Mosta -- Institute for the Creative Arts, Qormi/Luqa -- Institute of Applied Sciences' Centre of Agriculture, Aquatics and Animal Sciences, and Gozo), with roughly 6,500 full-time and 5,000 part-time students. MCAST's own About page confirms it offers over 190 full-time and 300+ part-time courses from MQF/EQF Level 1 to Level 8 (certificate through Doctoral), and holds self-accrediting status from the Malta National Commission for Higher and Further Education (referred to as MFHEA), with degrees aligned to the European Qualifications Framework. The Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing (course code AS6-W04-23) is MCAST's flagship first-level nursing qualification: a 3-year, full-time, 180-ECTS, MQF/EQF Level 6 degree administered by the Institute of Applied Sciences, requiring 4,600 total hours (2,300 theory + 2,300 supervised clinical placement) as stipulated by EU Directive 2005/36/EC, leading to eligibility to register with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta and, per MCAST's own page, recognition across all EU member states. A notable structural fact independently corroborated via Northumbria University's own official course page: MCAST previously ran (and may still formally list) a joint BSc (Hons) Nursing Studies dual-award and professional-registration programme with UK-based Northumbria University, involving Northumbria-registered academic staff, shared clinical-skills and simulation facilities at MCAST, and use of the Malta Nursing and Midwifery Council's 2020 code of ethics as a teaching framework. However, Northumbria's own page for this specific joint-award route explicitly states the course is 'no longer accepting new applications,' with the most recent listed start date being October 2021 -- meaning this specific UK dual-award pathway should NOT be assumed open, even though MCAST's own catalogue continues to list a standalone BSc (Hons) Nursing degree (AS6-W04-23) as active for the 2026/27 prospectus year. This draft is careful to present the MCAST standalone degree as the corroborated-active pathway and flags the Northumbria dual-award status as requiring direct verification. Separately, search-result summaries (not independently verified on a live page, since the source was blocked by a Cloudflare bot-challenge during this research pass) describe an MG2i-run 'Undergraduate Certificate in Nursing Studies (Bridging Course)' aimed at non-EU-qualified nurses who already hold a BSc in Nursing but need to close specific EU Directive 2005/36/EC learning-outcome gaps; MG2i is described on MCAST's own site as MCAST's commercial training arm. This bridging course's duration, current fees, and intake details could not be confirmed on a live official page in this pass and are flagged for direct reconfirmation. On the India-specific regulatory question: India's Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognises foreign nursing qualifications on a case-by-case, reciprocal-equivalence basis via its Equivalence Committee under Section 11(2)(a) of the INC Act 1947, rather than through any blanket, pre-approved country list; no INC statement specific to MCAST or to Malta-issued nursing qualifications was found during this research pass, so this draft does NOT assert automatic INC recognition and instead instructs students to apply directly to INC for equivalence assessment before relying on an MCAST degree for Indian nursing registration purposes. No tuition-fee figure for the BSc (Hons) Nursing programme (EU/Malta-domiciled or international/non-EU rate) could be corroborated on MCAST's own official pages during this research pass, so tuition is intentionally omitted from this draft rather than estimated; applicants must obtain current, category-specific fee information directly from MCAST's International Office. MCAST does not operate its own on-campus student housing; independent secondary sourcing (student-accommodation platforms and MCAST's own International Students page context) indicates MCAST's International Office assists students with off-campus private accommodation, with Paola itself (home to MCAST's Main Campus) and nearby Mosta offering a range of private student housing, supermarkets, shops, and restaurants -- but no MCAST-operated hostel or dedicated on-campus residence was corroborated, so this draft's hostel/accommodation narrative reflects a private off-campus support model rather than an institutional hostel. Paola itself is independently corroborated (Britannica; general Malta locality sourcing) as a historic town in Malta's South Eastern Region near the Grand Harbour area, today a residential and commercial hub; it is also home to the Hal Saflieni Hypogeum UNESCO World Heritage Neolithic site and other notable local landmarks per general independent Malta-tourism/reference sourcing, though this draft treats such general-interest facts cautiously and does not overstate their relevance to day-to-day student life. No dedicated Indian-student community or Indian-food data point specific to MCAST or Paola was corroborated in this research pass; the Indian food/community narrative is grounded conservatively in Malta's general, modest-but-present Indian dining scene (more concentrated in Sliema/St Julian's/Valletta than in Paola itself) rather than any MCAST- or Paola-specific claim.

draftContent.summary: MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology) is Malta's largest public vocational and higher-education institution, established in 2001 and headquartered at its Paola Main Campus, with additional institutes and campuses in Mosta, Qormi/Luqa, and Gozo. Among its degree programmes is the Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing, a 3-year, full-time, 180-ECTS, MQF/EQF Level 6 qualification delivered by MCAST's Institute of Applied Sciences. The programme requires 4,600 total hours (2,300 theoretical plus 2,300 supervised clinical-placement hours) as stipulated by EU Directive 2005/36/EC, and graduates who pass all units may apply for registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta, with MCAST stating the qualification is recognised across all EU member states. MCAST holds self-accrediting status from Malta's higher-education regulator, and instruction is entirely in English at a bilingual (Maltese-English) institution. Indian applicants should note that separate Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognition of any foreign nursing qualification, including one from MCAST, is assessed case-by-case through INC's own equivalence process rather than granted automatically, so this should be independently confirmed with INC before enrolling. MCAST also previously ran a joint dual-award route with UK partner Northumbria University, but Northumbria's own page for that specific route states it is no longer accepting new applications, so this page focuses on MCAST's own standalone Nursing degree, which remains listed as active in the current course catalogue.
draftContent.campusLifestyle: MCAST's Nursing degree sits within the Institute of Applied Sciences, one of the college's several specialised institutes spread across Paola (the Main Campus), Mosta, Qormi/Luqa, and Gozo. As a large, self-accrediting public vocational and higher-education institution with roughly 6,500 full-time students, MCAST offers a more technically and practically focused environment than a traditional research university, with strong industry links, hands-on clinical-skills facilities, and simulation-based teaching supplementing classroom learning. Nursing students combine campus-based theory with structured, mentor-supervised clinical placements at Malta and Gozo's healthcare providers, and MCAST's own course page emphasises evidence-based, holistic, patient-centred training aligned with EU nursing standards. Because MCAST spans multiple institutes and campuses, students should confirm with the Institute of Applied Sciences exactly which campus facilities (Paola or the Qormi/Luqa applied-sciences site) are used for specific teaching blocks and simulation sessions during their intake year.
draftContent.cityProfile: Paola is a long-established town in Malta's South Eastern Region, historically linked to Malta's Grand Harbour area and today functioning as a busy residential and commercial hub, home to landmarks such as the Hal Saflieni Hypogeum UNESCO World Heritage Neolithic site and the Basilica of Christ the King. As MCAST's Main Campus location, Paola gives students everyday access to local shops, markets, bakeries, and public transport connections rather than a purpose-built, isolated university-town setting. Malta as a whole is a compact, bilingual (Maltese and English) EU island nation roughly 100km from mainland Europe, with international flight connections through Malta International Airport and a Mediterranean climate of hot, dry summers and milder winters. Students can reach Valletta, Sliema, and other coastal towns quickly by bus, giving Paola-based MCAST students reasonably easy access to Malta's wider student and social scene while living in a more workaday, less tourist-oriented town.
draftContent.clinicalExposure: MCAST's official course page states that Nursing students complete a minimum of 4,600 hours over the 3-year programme, split evenly between 2,300 theoretical hours and 2,300 clinical-practice hours, as required by EU Directive 2005/36/EC. Most of the practice hours are carried out on clinical placements supervised by qualified nurse mentors, giving students direct, supervised exposure to patient care within Malta and Gozo's health system. Independent corroboration from Northumbria University's own description of its (now closed to new applicants) joint programme with MCAST describes clinical placement blocks run in close collaboration with Malta and Gozo's health service providers, classroom and clinical-skills-suite teaching, and simulation-based education (including simulated intravenous-therapy training) using MCAST's own facilities -- corroborating that MCAST's Nursing pathway generally combines academic coursework with structured hospital/community placements and mentor supervision, though applicants should request the current, detailed placement schedule and specific partner-facility list directly from MCAST's Institute of Applied Sciences before enrolling.
draftContent.hostelOverview: MCAST does not operate its own on-campus student hostel or residence; official MCAST material for international students does not describe an institutional housing product, and its International Office instead supports students in arranging off-campus private accommodation in Malta. Paola, home to MCAST's Main Campus, and nearby towns such as Mosta (near the Institute for the Creative Arts campus) both offer a range of private rental rooms, shared apartments, and student-oriented residences run by independent third-party operators, alongside everyday amenities like supermarkets and restaurants. Students should budget time to research and secure private accommodation well before arrival, ideally with guidance from MCAST's International Office, and should treat room availability, pricing, and lease terms as items to confirm directly with individual landlords or accommodation platforms rather than assuming a college-managed housing product exists.
draftContent.indianFoodSupport: Paola itself does not have a large, established Indian community or a wide range of dedicated Indian restaurants and grocery stores. Malta's more prominent dining and expatriate hubs -- such as Sliema, St Julian's, and Valletta -- have a modestly more developed Indian and South Asian food scene, reachable from Paola by bus or a short taxi ride. Because Malta does not have as large or long-established an Indian student population as some bigger European study destinations, students who rely on specific Indian ingredients or home-style cooking should expect to combine regular supermarket shopping with occasional trips to specialty stores in Malta's busier towns, and should connect with any existing Indian or South Asian student and community groups in Malta for current, first-hand guidance before arrival.
draftContent.safetyOverview: Malta is generally regarded as a safe, EU-regulated island nation with a comparatively low crime rate by broader European standards, and Paola's everyday, working-town character (rather than a nightlife-heavy district) generally supports a calmer day-to-day environment for students. As with any clinical-placement-based nursing programme, MCAST requires an occupational health-screening certificate and a clean police conduct certificate before students begin clinical practice, which is a relevant safety and safeguarding checkpoint specific to this course. Students should still follow standard personal-safety practices in any new city, ensure they meet Malta's visa and residence-registration requirements as international students, and stay in touch with the nearest Indian diplomatic mission covering Malta for updated consular guidance throughout their stay.
draftContent.studentSupport: MCAST's own International Students material describes a growing international student community across a broad range of English-medium programmes, including Nursing, supported by an International Office and a standard online-application process detailed on MCAST's How to Apply page. Nursing applicants specifically go through a values-based interview (with an added Preliminary Interview stage for international applicants) as part of MCAST's admissions process, alongside occupational health screening and police conduct clearance ahead of clinical placements. Given that some MCAST international-student material is not recently dated, and that a formerly available Northumbria University dual-award route for this Nursing degree is now closed to new applications per Northumbria's own page, Indian applicants should treat direct, current, written confirmation from MCAST's International Office and Institute of Applied Sciences as essential before applying -- covering current entry requirements, intake dates, fees, and the present status of any UK dual-award option -- rather than relying solely on older cached material.
whyChoose: A 3-year, 180-ECTS, MQF/EQF Level 6 Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing from MCAST, a public, self-accrediting institution whose degrees are aligned with the European Qualifications Framework and internationally recognised

A curriculum structured around EU Directive 2005/36/EC's 4,600-hour requirement (2,300 theory + 2,300 mentor-supervised clinical practice), giving graduates a pathway to registration with the Nursing and Midwifery Council of Malta and recognition across EU member states
English-medium instruction throughout, in a bilingual Maltese-English EU island setting that simplifies day-to-day communication for international students
Clinical placements delivered in close collaboration with Malta and Gozo's health service providers, supplemented by classroom-based clinical-skills suites and simulation facilities at MCAST
A compact, well-connected home base at MCAST's Paola Main Campus, close to Malta's wider bus network and within reach of Valletta, Sliema, and other coastal towns for broader student and social life
thingsToConsider: MCAST does not provide on-campus student housing; international applicants must independently arrange private off-campus accommodation in Paola or nearby towns, ideally with support from MCAST's International Office, well ahead of arrival

No tuition-fee figure for EU/Malta-domiciled or international/non-EU students could be confirmed on MCAST's own official pages during this research; applicants must obtain current, category-specific fee information directly from MCAST's International Office before budgeting

MCAST previously offered a joint BSc (Hons) Nursing Studies dual-award with UK partner Northumbria University, but Northumbria's own course page states this specific route is no longer accepting new applications (last listed intake October 2021); applicants wanting a UK dual-award option specifically must verify current status directly with MCAST rather than assuming it remains open

A separate, shorter Nursing Studies Bridging Course for non-EU-qualified nurses is reported to run via MG2i (MCAST's commercial arm), but its live official page could not be accessed during this research (blocked by a bot-security challenge); its duration, curriculum, fees, and intake dates should be reconfirmed directly with MG2i/MCAST before applying

Indian Nursing Council (INC) recognition of an MCAST nursing qualification is not automatic; INC assesses foreign nursing qualifications case-by-case through its own equivalence process, so applicants should apply to INC directly (or seek current guidance) before assuming an MCAST degree will convert smoothly into Indian nursing registration

Admission requires a multi-stage process, including a values-based interview (plus a Preliminary Interview for international applicants), an occupational health-screening certificate, and a clean police conduct certificate, which applicants should prepare for well in advance
Paola itself has limited Indian food and community infrastructure; students wanting that specifically should expect to travel to busier Maltese towns like Sliema, St Julian's, or Valletta

faq.question: Is MCAST a public or private institution, and when was it established?
faq.answer: MCAST (Malta College of Arts, Science and Technology) is a public vocational and higher-education institution, established in 2001, per MCAST's own About page and independent corroboration on Wikipedia. It is Malta's largest institution of its kind, headquartered at its Paola Main Campus with additional institutes and campuses in Mosta, Qormi/Luqa, and Gozo.

faq.question: What does the MCAST Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing involve, and how long does it take?
faq.answer: It is a 3-year, full-time, 180-ECTS, MQF/EQF Level 6 degree delivered by MCAST's Institute of Applied Sciences, requiring a minimum of 4,600 hours over the three years -- 2,300 theoretical hours and 2,300 supervised clinical-practice hours -- per EU Directive 2005/36/EC, with most practice hours completed on clinical placements supervised by qualified nurse mentors, per MCAST's own official course page.

faq.question: Will this MCAST Nursing degree be recognised by India's Indian Nursing Council (INC)?
faq.answer: This is not confirmed, and this page does not assert automatic recognition. India's Indian Nursing Council evaluates foreign nursing qualifications on a case-by-case, reciprocal-equivalence basis through its own Equivalence Committee process, rather than through a pre-approved blanket list of recognised countries or institutions. No INC statement specific to MCAST or Malta-issued nursing qualifications was found during this research, so applicants should apply directly to INC for an equivalence assessment, and get current written guidance, before relying on an MCAST degree for nursing registration in India.

faq.question: Is there still a joint UK dual-award option with Northumbria University for this Nursing degree?
faq.answer: MCAST has previously run a joint BSc (Hons) Nursing Studies dual-award and professional-registration programme with UK-based Northumbria University. However, Northumbria's own official course page for this specific joint-award route states that it is no longer accepting new applications, with the most recently listed start date being October 2021. MCAST's own standalone BSc (Hons) Nursing degree remains listed as active in its current course catalogue, so applicants specifically interested in a UK dual-award option should contact MCAST directly to confirm whether, and in what form, that route is currently available.

faq.question: What are the entry requirements for MCAST's Nursing degree, and what does the admissions process involve?
faq.answer: Per MCAST's official course page, entry requires either specific MCAST Advanced Diplomas (in Health Sciences, Health and Social Care, Social Care, Dental Surgery Assistants, or Pharmacy Technicians) or 2 A-Level plus 2 I-Level passes, with a Maturity Clause route available for applicants aged 23 and over who lack these qualifications but can demonstrate relevant achievements. All applicants must pass a values-based interview (international applicants first go through a Preliminary Interview), and must later provide an occupational health-screening certificate and a clean police conduct certificate before starting clinical placements.

faq.question: Does MCAST offer a shorter bridging course for nurses who already have a nursing degree?
faq.answer: Search-result summaries describe an Undergraduate Certificate in Nursing Studies (Bridging Course), reportedly around 6 months long and run via MG2i (described on MCAST's own site as its commercial arm), intended for non-EU-qualified nurses who already hold a BSc in Nursing but need to cover specific learning-outcome gaps required under EU Directive 2005/36/EC. This page's research pass could not independently verify these details on a live official page due to a bot-security block, so applicants should treat this as a lead to independently confirm -- covering exact duration, curriculum, fees, and entry requirements -- directly with MG2i or MCAST before applying.

faq.question: Does MCAST provide student housing, and where do Nursing students typically live?
faq.answer: MCAST does not operate its own on-campus student hostel or residence. Its International Office supports students in finding private, off-campus accommodation, and Paola (home to MCAST's Main Campus) along with nearby towns such as Mosta offer a range of independently run private rentals and student housing options. Applicants should start researching accommodation early and confirm current pricing and availability directly with private providers or MCAST's International Office.

faq.question: How much does the MCAST Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Nursing cost for international students?
faq.answer: No specific tuition-fee figure for EU/Malta-domiciled or international/non-EU students could be confirmed on MCAST's own official pages during this research pass. Applicants must contact MCAST's International Office directly for current, category-specific fee information before budgeting for the programme.
